pattern: A DP over (node, edges used) tracking the set of achievable sums below the threshold finds the largest k-edge path weight that stays strictly under t.
hints: Only path weights below t are ever useful, and weights are tiny, so the reachable sums per node form a small set bounded by t.
Process edges in layers: after e relaxations you know, for each node, which path-sums under t are reachable using exactly e edges.
Iterate e from 1 to k; for each node u and each reachable sum s, extend along edges (u,v,w) keeping only s+w < t; after k layers take the max reachable sum, or -1 if none.

edge_inputs.description: single two-edge chain under the threshold
no path stays strictly below t, return -1
k=0 means a single node, weight 0 < t
k exceeds longest path length, no k-edge path exists
branching, must pick the heavier branch under t

stress.description: random DAG (edges from lower to higher index) with small weights
